metadata.name: LeadBridge
metadata.description: LeadBridge is a multi-tenant, role-scoped lead management platform designed for managing leads across multiple zones, areas, and enterprise tenants. It implements a three-tier hierarchical access control model with JWT-based authentication via Keycloak.

objectives.objective: Centralize lead management across multiple geographic zones and areas
Enforce role-based data visibility and access control
Provide zone managers and area managers independent dashboards scoped to their jurisdiction
Enable master admin to view and manage all leads, zones, and areas globally
Send notifications and emails via dedicated email service

service.name: Discovery Server (eureka-server)
service.purpose: Service registry and discovery for dynamic service lookup

service.name: API Gateway
service.purpose: Single entry point for all client requests; routes to backend services

service.name: Lead Service
service.purpose: Core CRUD operations for leads with role-aware query filtering

service.name: Tenant Service
service.purpose: Manage zones and areas (tenants); role-based visibility

service.name: Common Library
service.purpose: Shared utilities, exceptions, context, and DTOs used by all services

key_components.component: TenantContext - ThreadLocal storage for role, tenantId, msspId
AppException - Base exception class for consistent error handling

external.purpose: Identity provider and OAuth2/OIDC server for authentication
Persistent data store for leads, tenants, users

frontend.purpose: SPA for user interaction, role-scoped dashboard routing

accessControl.model: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with JWT Claims

access.data: All leads across all zones and areas
access.operations: Create/Read/Update/Delete leads, zones, and areas globally
access.dashboard: /dashboard/master - Global view with all zones and areas

access.data: All leads in areas under their zone
access.operations: Read leads in zone, view zone report, cannot create/delete

seed_users.user: hyd-zone / hyd123 - Manages Hyderabad zone (Abids, Attapur areas)
sec-zone / sec123 - Manages Secunderabad zone (Ameerpet, Secunderabad areas)

access.data: Leads only in their assigned area
access.operations: Create/Read/Update/Delete leads within area; cannot delete
access.dashboard: /dashboard/area - Area-scoped view with lead management forms

seed_users.user: abids-area / abids123 - Abids area under Hyderabad zone
attapur-area / attapur123 - Attapur area under Hyderabad zone
ameerpet-area / ameerpet123 - Ameerpet area under Secunderabad zone
secunderabad-area / secarea123 - Secunderabad area under Secunderabad zone

enforcement.method: JwtTenantFilter extracts realm_access.roles[], tenantId, msspId claims from JWT token
enforcement.storage: Stores in ThreadLocal TenantContext for per-request scope
enforcement.cleanup: Filter finally block clears TenantContext to prevent thread pool leaks
enforcement.authorization: Services validate TenantContext.getRole() before executing queries

error_semantics.when: Missing required tenantId/msspId claims in token
User role not authorized for operation (e.g., MSSP trying to create lead)
Lead exists but user's role cannot access it (scoping permission denied)

frontend.styling: CSS3 with CSS Variables for theming

attributes.attribute: status - NEW, CONTACTED, QUALIFIED, CONVERTED, REJECTED

entity.name: Tenant (Zone/Area)
entity.scope: Hierarchical: Zone (MSSP parent) contains Areas (ENTERPRISE_TENANT children)

feature.name: Role-Scoped Lead Management
feature.description: Every lead query is filtered by user's role and tenant context. MASTER sees all, MSSP sees zone, ENTERPRISE_TENANT sees area only.
feature.implementation: LeadService.getLeads() uses TenantContext.getRole() to dispatch to appropriate repository method

feature.name: Zone Dashboard (MSSP)
feature.description: Zone managers see aggregated metrics across all areas in their zone: total leads, areas count, conversion rate

feature.name: Area Dashboard (ENTERPRISE_TENANT)
feature.description: Area managers see their assigned area's leads with status management and add-lead form

feature.name: Master Dashboard (MASTER_MSSP)
feature.description: Global view of all zones, areas, and leads; can view and manage anything
feature.implementation: frontend/dashboard.component; full data access via all report endpoints

feature.name: Keycloak Integration
feature.description: JWT-based authentication with OAuth2 authorization code flow; claims extracted for tenantId, msspId, areaName
feature.implementation: Keycloak realm-export.json defines users, roles, protocol mappers; JwtTenantFilter parses JWT

feature.name: Email Notifications
feature.description: Async email service for lead notifications and alerts
feature.implementation: leadbridge-email-service with async message queue integration

feature.name: Service Discovery
feature.description: Eureka-based service registry for dynamic service URLs and health checking
feature.implementation: Discovery server at port 8761; all services register and deregister

completed.item: ✅ TenantContext with role, tenantId, msspId ThreadLocal storage
✅ JwtTenantFilter extracting and populating context from JWT claims
✅ Lead repository with role-aware query methods (findAll, findByTenantId, findByTenantIdIn)
✅ LeadService with role-scoped business logic (MASTER→all, MSSP→zone, TENANT→area)
✅ ReportService with role-based filtering (global, mssp, tenant)
✅ Frontend AuthStateService with Angular signals for auth state
✅ Zone and Area dashboards with modern UI (hero shell, metric cards)
✅ Keycloak realm configuration with 7 seeded users and protocol mappers
✅ Docker compose setup with all 7 services and infrastructure

in_progress.item: 🟠 Docker build verification (lead-service Maven compile patch recently applied)

future.item: Test role-scoped data visibility across all logins

user.access: Global (all zones, all areas, all leads)

file.purpose: ThreadLocal context holder for role, tenantId, msspId
JWT claim extraction and TenantContext population
Central auth state with Angular signals (role, tenantId, msspId)
Zone manager dashboard with modern UI
Area manager dashboard with lead management
Keycloak realm config with users, roles, protocol mappers

quick_start.step: Navigate to infrastructure folder: cd infrastructure
Wait for services to be healthy (2-3 minutes)
Login with any test credentials (see testing_credentials section)
Verify role-scoped dashboard loads correct data for role
Test data visibility by switching between different user roles

note.content: All requests pass through JwtTenantFilter which validates JWT and populates TenantContext. Missing or invalid tokens result in 401 UNAUTHORIZED. Role mismatches result in 403 FORBIDDEN or 404 NOT_FOUND (scoped resource not visible).
PostgreSQL seed data includes leads distributed across areas. Lead counts align with seeded area tenantId values. TenantContext filtering ensures no cross-tenant data leakage.
realm-export.json is imported during Keycloak startup. Users have tenantId, msspId, areaName attributes mapped to JWT claims via protocol mappers on leadbridge-frontend client.

AuthStateService initializes during APP_INITIALIZER phase after Keycloak.init() completes. All auth state reads use signals (currentRole, currentTenantId, currentMsspId). RoleGuard prevents template compilation if user lacks required role.
